title: INNER JOIN witnesses in spendable_notes() — semantically correct, worth defending
description: spendable_notes() uses INNER JOIN witnesses rather than LEFT JOIN + IS NOT NULL. This means a note with no witness row is genuinely not spendable (no Merkle proof = no inclusion proof = builder panics). A contributor might suggest LEFT JOIN to 'show all notes' and let the caller handle missing witnesses. Do not do that: the JOIN is the semantic contract. If a note has no witness, it is not a spendable note and has no business being in the result set. The current design prevents a class of panics.
close_reason: decision records documented in beads; no code change required
